steps.title: Apply the one-solution discriminant condition
steps.content:
A quadratic equation $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$ has exactly one solution when its discriminant equals zero.
In $-16x^2 - 8x + c = 0$, we have $a = -16$ and $b = -8$.

steps.title: Solve for c using the discriminant equation
steps.content:
Substitute $a = -16$, $b = -8$, and the unknown $c$ into the discriminant equation:
$$(-8)^2 - 4(-16)(c) = 0$$
$$64 + 64c = 0$$
$$c = -1$$
